name: Agent Access Control Template
description: Automated workflow: Agent Access Control Template. This workflow integrates 17 different services: telegramTrigger, stickyNote, airtable, code, toolHttpRequest. It contains 45 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

parameters.text: =Unknown user '{{ $('Telegram Trigger').item.json.message.from.username }}'. Please contact your supervisor.

parameters.content:
## Choose Base
Copy [this Airtable Template]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} into your workspace and select that Base here

## Choose workflow
Select the current workflow as the workflow that should be called
Uses different session ID (suffix), since every agent needs its own memory
Listens to messages directly sent to the bot
Checks if the user was found in Airtable
Uses list of allowed tools gathered from Airtable to check for permissions and replaces denied tools with a fixed instruction to return a message to the user.
Tools can also be connected without a permission check
Main agent with the instruction to always use the connected tools instead of general knowledge
Collects input and formats it using required keys
Be aware, that previous responses may still exist after permission changes
Collects and formats input from parent workflow / agent
Uses the same technique as the main agent to check for permissions using the same parameters which were passed to this sub-agent.
Sub-agent with specific role to handle weather information. Also having the instruction to strictly only use the connected tools.

options.systemMessage:
=You are a weather data assistant.
You MUST only use the provided tools to process any user input. Never use general knowledge to answer questions. If you can't use a tool, tell the user why.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details

Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set input".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Settings".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
